title: Liquid, Stack, Wave, Spring, Swipe
description: Five live UI demos: SVG ink-fill buttons, sticky scroll panel stacking, variable font wave, elastic cursor chain, and a swipe card stack.

drops.title: Liquid Ink Fill Button
drops.description: SVG filter magic makes buttons fill with organic ink from wherever your cursor lands.
drops.concept: Uses an inline SVG filter chain (feTurbulence → feDisplacementMap → feGaussianBlur → feColorMatrix goo matrix) applied as a clip-path expander on button hover. The filter's fractalNoise distorts the expanding circle into an organic ink-blot shape. The origin point is tracked via JS to always start from the cursor position.
drops.source: Lucas Bebber · CSS-Tricks goo effect + Codrops liquid button tutorials

interaction.mouse: hover button to see ink fill from cursor position

drops.title: Stacked Scroll Panels
drops.description: Sticky panels that peel away as you scroll, each revealing the next in a smooth layered wipe.
drops.concept: Each section uses position:sticky so all panels stack at the top of the viewport simultaneously. A cover element (div with background matching the page) uses CSS scaleY and transform-origin:bottom to wipe upward as the user scrolls into each panel's scroll segment. A progress bar provides orientation. No JavaScript libraries needed — only rAF-free scroll listeners.
drops.source: Andy Bell · Piccalilli sticky scroll stacking technique, 2025

drops.title: Variable Font Weight Wave
drops.description: A sine wave sweeps through per-character font-weight, making type flow like liquid.
drops.concept: Splits each word into individual span elements and drives their CSS font-weight (100–900) using a sin() wave offset by character index and time. The wave phase shifts across both words and characters simultaneously, creating an undulating 3D-depth illusion. Three color modes (white, per-word color, rainbow) are selectable from a debug panel. Requires a variable font — system-ui covers most browsers.
drops.source: Mandy Michael · Variable Fonts for Responsive Design, CSS Day 2019 (technique popularized 2024–2026)

interaction.mouse: hover words — debug panel controls wave parameters
interaction.touch: touch to view; debug panel adjustable

drops.title: Elastic Chain Cursor
drops.description: A spring-physics bead chain trails the cursor — each node chasing the previous with lag.
drops.concept: A Canvas 2D chain of N nodes where node[0] chases the real cursor using spring physics (velocity += (target - pos) * stiffness; velocity *= damping), and each subsequent node chases the one before it with a slightly weaker spring constant. The result is an elastic, rubber-band feel. A DOM ring element tracks node[0] for crisp pointer feedback. Configurable via a debug panel.
drops.source: Yoichi Kobayashi · spring cursor chain concept (widely implemented on codepen/awwwards sites 2025–2026)

interaction.mouse: move cursor to drive the chain; hover .hot elements for reactive ring

drops.title: Swipe Card Stack
drops.description: Pointer-drag card stack with velocity throw, LIKE/NOPE labels, and three action buttons.
drops.concept: Cards stack via absolute positioning + decreasing scale/Y-offset for depth. The top card captures pointer events; drag distance drives rotation (dx * 0.06 degrees) and progressively fades in LIKE or NOPE labels. Releasing past an 80px threshold triggers a flyOut animation (translateX offscreen + rotate + opacity:0). Action buttons programmatically trigger the same animation. On depletion, a restore button rebuilds the stack.
drops.source: Tinder card swipe pattern — widely reimplemented; reference: Formidable Labs/Shufflr (2024)

interaction.click: action buttons to discard or super-like
interaction.touch: swipe left/right to like or nope
